items.caption: A slide from a presentation on access control systems. it features two distinct scenarios of access control in action. on the left, a person is seen using a key card to unlock a door, which is labeled as "the ubiquitous employee badge is rfid." this suggests that the key card is an rfid-enabled badge, a common method of access control in many organizations. on the right, there's a photograph of a vehicle approaching a checkpoint, where a red truck is being inspected. the checkpoint is labeled as "vehicle access - nexus border inspection program at peace arch." this indicates that the vehicle is passing through a border inspection program, likely at the peace arch border crossing, which is part of the nexus program for expedited border crossings. the slide is set against a blue background with white text, and there's a gold-colored bar at the top.
